benchmarks.description: Return-only baseline
benchmarks.hypothesis: Captures the staged harness floor for baseline subtraction
benchmarks.name: simple

benchmarks.description: Packet-backed return-only baseline
benchmarks.hypothesis: Removes staged input helper overhead to expose the packet-backed harness floor
benchmarks.name: simple_packet

baseline_adjustment.reason: skipped: benchmark io_mode packet does not match baseline io_mode staged
skipped: benchmark io_mode packet does not match baseline io_mode staged

benchmarks.description: Fixed-size two-word memory baseline
benchmarks.hypothesis: Minimal fixed-width load pair with almost no control-flow
benchmarks.name: memory_pair_sum

benchmarks.description: Kernighan popcount over generated u64 input
benchmarks.hypothesis: Favors stronger scalar optimization and loop code generation
benchmarks.name: bitcount

benchmarks.description: Fold log2 over generated u64 input
benchmarks.hypothesis: Mixes compares and bit tests with modest control-flow
benchmarks.name: log2_fold

benchmarks.description: Short serialized dependency chain over generated u64 input
benchmarks.hypothesis: Long scalar dependencies reduce ILP and stress single-chain scheduling
benchmarks.name: dep_chain_short

benchmarks.description: Long serialized dependency chain over generated u64 input
benchmarks.hypothesis: Scaling dependency depth should amplify backend scheduling differences
benchmarks.name: dep_chain_long

benchmarks.description: Repeated search over sorted data
benchmarks.hypothesis: Branch-heavy search should reward stronger branch-oriented code generation
benchmarks.name: binary_search

benchmarks.description: Hot/cold branch skew for layout experiments
benchmarks.hypothesis: Skewed branches should expose layout and prediction quality differences
benchmarks.name: branch_layout

benchmarks.description: Dense switch dispatch over generated 6-bit values
benchmarks.hypothesis: Dense dispatch should reflect jump-table or compare-ladder lowering quality
benchmarks.name: switch_dispatch

benchmarks.description: High-density conditional branch mesh over generated u32 inputs
benchmarks.hypothesis: Many short conditional paths should magnify branch lowering and layout quality
benchmarks.name: branch_dense

benchmarks.description: Repeated single-diamond value selection over staged compare/select arrays
benchmarks.hypothesis: Narrow assignment-only diamonds should expose whether the backend can lower hot selects without branch overhead
benchmarks.name: cmov_select

benchmarks.description: 32 inlined compare/select sites over staged u64 arrays
benchmarks.hypothesis: Many inlined select sites should magnify any kernel JIT benefit from branch-to-cmov lowering
benchmarks.name: cmov_dense

benchmarks.description: Internet checksum style reduction
benchmarks.hypothesis: Sequential 16-bit reductions should emphasize scalar memory throughput
benchmarks.name: checksum

benchmarks.description: Direct 32-bit loads over staged input words
benchmarks.hypothesis: Direct u32 loads should surface kernel byte-recompose lowering overhead relative to llvmbpf
benchmarks.name: load_word32

benchmarks.description: Same staged input walked as individual byte loads
benchmarks.hypothesis: Byte-at-a-time control should avoid kernel byte-recompose while preserving the same staged input footprint
benchmarks.name: load_byte

benchmarks.description: Recompose 128 staged u64 values from individual byte loads
benchmarks.hypothesis: Kernel byte-load lowering should add measurable exec cost versus equivalent native u64 loads
benchmarks.name: load_byte_recompose

benchmarks.description: Read the same 128 staged u64 values via native aligned u64 loads
benchmarks.hypothesis: Native u64 loads provide the control path for isolating kernel byte-recompose overhead
benchmarks.name: load_native_u64

benchmarks.description: Packet header parsing with bounds checks
benchmarks.hypothesis: Structured parsing with field extraction may favor kernel's packet-oriented JIT path
benchmarks.name: packet_parse

benchmarks.description: Record parsing with dense layered bounds checks
benchmarks.hypothesis: Dense bounds checks should stress compare-heavy code generation more than raw memory bandwidth
benchmarks.name: bounds_ladder

benchmarks.description: Record parser with dependent bounds checks and switch-based fixed-offset loads
benchmarks.hypothesis: Dense bounds guards plus selector-driven fixed-offset dispatch should expose compare chains, branch pressure, and critical-path inflation
benchmarks.name: bounds_check_heavy

benchmarks.description: Sequential-stride load pattern with stride 4 over a ring buffer
benchmarks.hypothesis: Mild stride should stay cache-friendly while exposing address-generation cost
benchmarks.name: stride_load_4

benchmarks.description: Wider-stride load pattern with stride 16 over a ring buffer
benchmarks.hypothesis: Wider stride should amplify addressing and prefetch sensitivity
benchmarks.name: stride_load_16

benchmarks.description: Interleaved ALU and irregular memory operations over a shared ring
benchmarks.hypothesis: Alternating compute and memory should separate scalar scheduling gains from pure load bandwidth
benchmarks.name: mixed_alu_mem

benchmarks.description: Multi-accumulator arithmetic intended to stress register allocation
benchmarks.hypothesis: Register pressure should expose spill/fill quality and register allocator limits
benchmarks.name: spill_pressure

benchmarks.description: Four-lane accumulator kernel with cross-lane mixing
benchmarks.hypothesis: More independent accumulators should reward ILP-friendly code generation
benchmarks.name: multi_acc_4

benchmarks.description: Eight-lane accumulator kernel with cross-lane mixing
benchmarks.hypothesis: Higher lane count should trade ILP gains against register pressure
benchmarks.name: multi_acc_8

benchmarks.description: Loop-heavy Fibonacci-style recurrence with bounded iteration count
benchmarks.hypothesis: Scalar recurrence loops should narrow the gap by limiting exploitable ILP
benchmarks.name: fibonacci_iter

benchmarks.description: Packet-backed Fibonacci-style recurrence with bounded iteration count
benchmarks.hypothesis: Removes staged input helper overhead to isolate the packet-fed recurrence loop shape
benchmarks.name: fibonacci_iter_packet

baseline_adjustment.reason: skipped: benchmark io_mode packet does not match baseline io_mode staged
skipped: benchmark io_mode packet does not match baseline io_mode staged

benchmarks.description: Short fixed-trip-count recurrence loop
benchmarks.hypothesis: Short fixed loops should keep loop overhead visible relative to the loop body
benchmarks.name: fixed_loop_small

benchmarks.description: Long fixed-trip-count recurrence loop
benchmarks.hypothesis: Larger trip counts should amortize setup overhead and highlight steady-state loop code quality
benchmarks.name: fixed_loop_large

benchmarks.description: Two-level bounded nested loop over a generated value grid
benchmarks.hypothesis: Two-level loop nests should expose loop-carried dependence handling beyond flat recurrences
benchmarks.name: nested_loop_2

benchmarks.description: Three-level bounded nested loop over a generated value grid
benchmarks.hypothesis: Deeper loop nesting should stress induction-variable handling and loop-exit code quality
benchmarks.name: nested_loop_3

benchmarks.description: Two-clone repeated scalar block inside the steady-state loop
benchmarks.hypothesis: Modest cloned straight-line code should slightly favor backends with stronger instruction scheduling
benchmarks.name: code_clone_2

benchmarks.description: Eight-clone repeated scalar block inside the steady-state loop
benchmarks.hypothesis: Larger cloned bodies should magnify code-size and straight-line scheduling differences
benchmarks.name: code_clone_8

benchmarks.description: Large mixed straight-line body targeting roughly 500 BPF instructions
benchmarks.hypothesis: Larger hot bodies should begin to surface I-cache and allocator scalability effects
benchmarks.name: large_mixed_500

benchmarks.description: Very large mixed straight-line body targeting roughly 1000 BPF instructions
benchmarks.hypothesis: Very large bodies should amplify backend scalability differences hidden by tiny kernels
benchmarks.name: large_mixed_1000

benchmarks.description: Chain of 3 __noinline BPF-to-BPF subprogram calls per loop iteration
benchmarks.hypothesis: Subprogram call overhead should differ between kernel callee-saved convention and LLVM direct calls
benchmarks.name: bpf_call_chain

benchmarks.description: Compare three 64-byte candidates against a fixed prefix pattern
benchmarks.hypothesis: Early-exit byte comparisons should expose byte-load lowering and branch short-circuit behavior
benchmarks.name: memcmp_prefix_64

benchmarks.description: Packet-backed Ethernet plus VLAN plus IPv4 plus TCP parser with options walk
benchmarks.hypothesis: Variable cursor advancement and repeated bounds checks should magnify packet-parse codegen differences
benchmarks.name: packet_parse_vlans_tcpopts

skipped: benchmark io_mode packet does not match baseline io_mode staged

benchmarks.description: Dispatch among four __noinline local subprograms with different pressure profiles
benchmarks.hypothesis: Fan-out local calls should stress call-site layout, prologue diversity, and join-point register allocation
benchmarks.name: local_call_fanout

benchmarks.description: Parse an IPv4 TCP 5-tuple and compute a Jenkins-style flow hash
benchmarks.hypothesis: Endian conversion plus field extraction and rotates should be more workload-realistic than standalone checksum math
benchmarks.name: packet_rss_hash

baseline_adjustment.reason: skipped: benchmark io_mode packet does not match baseline io_mode staged
skipped: benchmark io_mode packet does not match baseline io_mode staged

benchmarks.description: Straight-line reduction with many distinct 64-bit immediates
benchmarks.hypothesis: Dense literal materialization should surface backend differences hidden by data-driven kernels
benchmarks.name: imm64_storm

benchmarks.description: Alternate u32 and u64 arithmetic on the same live values
benchmarks.hypothesis: Mixed-width arithmetic should stress zero-extension correctness and 32-bit lowering quality
benchmarks.name: alu32_64_pingpong

benchmarks.description: Thirty-two-way sparse branch fanout with nontrivial per-arm work
benchmarks.hypothesis: Wide sparse fanout should stress branch layout and compare-ladder lowering beyond dense switch dispatch
benchmarks.name: branch_fanout_32

benchmarks.description: Eight-level nested guard tree over staged record fields
benchmarks.hypothesis: Deeply nested conditionals should magnify branch inversion, hot-path asymmetry, and join pressure
benchmarks.name: deep_guard_tree_8

benchmarks.description: Single very large acyclic straight-line block with many live temporaries
benchmarks.hypothesis: A huge basic block should directly stress scheduling and register allocation scalability
benchmarks.name: mega_basic_block_2048

benchmarks.description: SipHash-like hash using 64-bit rotations to stress the ROTATE rewrite
benchmarks.hypothesis: Dense 64-bit rotations should exercise the 4-insn rotate rewrite pattern
benchmarks.name: rotate64_hash

benchmarks.description: Packet-backed record parser with one wide dominating window plus many redundant sub-checks
benchmarks.hypothesis: Dominating range checks should create a clean target for redundant bounds-check elimination
benchmarks.name: packet_redundant_bounds

benchmarks.description: ALU32-to-ALU64 constant cascade with repeated truncation and immediate updates
benchmarks.hypothesis: Cross-instruction constant folding should shrink constant-heavy ALU32 and ALU64 cascades
benchmarks.name: const_fold_chain

benchmarks.description: Dense fixed-offset field extraction from a staged array of naturally aligned records
benchmarks.hypothesis: Many same-base fixed-offset loads should reward base-plus-offset fusion and grouped field lowering
benchmarks.name: struct_field_cluster

benchmarks.description: Packed-word bitfield extraction over staged two-word records
benchmarks.hypothesis: Repeated constant shift-and-mask extracts should reward compact canonical extract lowering over packed inputs
benchmarks.name: bitfield_extract

benchmarks.description: Hot loop dominated by multiply-by-small-constant arithmetic and index scaling
benchmarks.hypothesis: Small constant multiplies should isolate LEA-style fusion and strength-reduction opportunities
benchmarks.name: smallmul_strength_reduce

benchmarks.description: Pure isolated compare/select diamonds over aligned staged arrays
benchmarks.hypothesis: Isolates COND_SELECT lowering without rotate or byte-recompose interference
benchmarks.name: cond_select_dense

benchmarks.description: Pure register-resident ARX rounds over native-loaded seed words
benchmarks.hypothesis: Isolates ROTATE lowering by keeping the hot path entirely in registers after seed loads
benchmarks.name: rotate_dense

benchmarks.description: Pure explicit address-generation stride walk over aligned u64 values
benchmarks.hypothesis: Isolates ADDR_CALC lowering via integer-address construction followed by u64 loads
benchmarks.name: addr_calc_stride

benchmarks.description: Pure packed-word bitfield extraction over aligned u64 staged input
benchmarks.hypothesis: Isolates BITFIELD_EXTRACT lowering using native loads and constant shift-and-mask fields
benchmarks.name: extract_dense

benchmarks.description: Pure aligned load plus byte-swap sequence over staged u32 input
benchmarks.hypothesis: Isolates ENDIAN_FUSION lowering by using native aligned loads followed by __builtin_bswap32
benchmarks.name: endian_swap_dense

benchmarks.description: Pure dense if/else diamonds over aligned staged u64 input
benchmarks.hypothesis: Isolates BRANCH_FLIP lowering with eight simple ALU-only diamonds repeated across 32 groups
benchmarks.name: branch_flip_dense

benchmarks.description: TC version of Kernighan popcount over generated u64 input
benchmarks.hypothesis: Reuses the staged popcount kernel under a sched_cls hook to validate non-XDP packet execution
benchmarks.name: tc_bitcount

benchmarks.description: TC version of the checksum reduction benchmark
benchmarks.hypothesis: Reuses the checksum reduction under a sched_cls hook with packet-fed staged input and skb-context result extraction
benchmarks.name: tc_checksum

benchmarks.description: cgroup_skb egress hash-chain reduction over generated u64 words
benchmarks.hypothesis: Exercises a non-XDP cgroup_skb hook with packet-fed staged input and skb-context result extraction
benchmarks.name: cgroup_hash_chain
